data.title: Where Texas slots in the second College Football Playoff rankings
data.description: Coming out of the season’s second and final by week, the Texas Longhorns moved up to No. 10 in the second release of the College Football Playoff rankings, benefitting from a loss by the BYU Cougars to the Texas Tech Red Raiders.The handy defeat by the Red Raiders in Lubbock dropped the Cougars from No. 7 to No. 12 as Texas Tech moved from No. 8 to No. 6. The main storyline for head coach Steve Sarkisian’s program in its quest to make a third straight playoff appearance is that the Horns remain in control of their own destiny heading into a high-profile tilt against the No. 5 Bulldogs in...

data.title: City Council approves three-year public transit contract
data.description: DeKALB — The DeKalb City Council met Monday to discuss the city’s 2025 property tax levy, public transportation contracts and funding to support local housing programs.A Truth in Taxation “Black Box” opened the meeting, discussing the 2025 property tax levy. The proposed corporate levy totals $8.98 million, representing a 7.65% increase from 2024. The DeKalb Public Library’s proposed levy also showed a 7.65% increase, which makes the combined total amount $13.38 million between the library and city levies.The council also held a public hearing regarding the Community Development Block...

data.author: Lily Meline, Opinions Columnist
data.title: Opinion | Left out: ‘Handism’ is real
data.description: A couple of years ago, I walked in on my left-handed mother attempting to cut a strip of fabric with right-handed scissors and silently cursing to herself. “We live in a world designed by right-handed people,” she told me bitterly. I, myself, am right-handed, so although I sympathized with her struggles, her contempt did feel a little dramatic. Sure, I recognize life is undoubtedly harder for left-handed people, but the way my mother –– and several other left-handed people I’ve met –– describe their struggles makes it sound as if they’re more discriminated against than they...
